meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Git with AI: Debugging Strategy for Student Developers
content.seo_description: Master debugging with Git and AI. An intermediate checklist for students to solve complex code issues using Antigravity and Claude Code for faster iteration.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at red error logs. Use Git version control as a sandbox to isolate bugs, then leverage Claude Code to analyze diffs and Antigravity to simulate edge cases. By systematically branching before debugging, you preserve your progress while using AI agents to pinpoint the exact line of failure.
content.intro:
You’re staring at the terminal. A wall of stack trace errors mocks your progress, and every attempt to 'fix' the code seems to birth three new bugs. The frustration of being stuck isn't a sign of failure; it's the inevitable initiation rite of every senior engineer who once stood exactly where you are now.

But the era of manual, blind debugging is over. By integrating Git’s immutable history with the raw reasoning power of Claude Code and the predictive modeling of Antigravity, you can transform your workflow from a guessing game into a surgical operation. It is time to stop fighting your code and start engineering your way out.

steps.title: Establish the 'Known-Good' Baseline
steps.description: Before touching broken code, create a 'debug' branch. This ensures you can always revert to a functional state, allowing the AI to analyze your changes without the fear of losing progress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current file structure and identify the most likely modules causing the recent regression based on the last 3 commits.
steps.claude_code_command: git checkout -b debug-session && git add . && git commit -m 'checkpoint before debugging'

steps.title: Contextualize the Error with Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code to read your current directory and the specific error log to gain full context of the environment and dependency issues.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the architectural conflict between these two modules that leads to the current runtime error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the error log in error.log and suggest the root cause in the codebase'

steps.title: Simulate Edge Cases
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate inputs that push your function to its limits, revealing hidden bugs that standard unit tests might miss.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of boundary condition test cases for this specific function to identify potential overflow or null pointer exceptions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test script that exercises the identified edge cases'

steps.title: Isolate and Patch
steps.description: Apply the fix in a dedicated commit. This allows you to verify the fix independently before merging back into your main development branch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor for this block that adheres to clean code principles and resolves the identified runtime error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the suggested fix to the codebase and verify with the test suite'

steps.title: Verify and Commit
steps.description: Validate the fix against your test suite. Once confirmed, squash your debug commits to keep a clean, professional history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the final implementation for any security vulnerabilities or performance bottlenecks introduced by the fix.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'Fix: Resolved runtime error with AI-assisted patch' && git checkout main && git merge debug-session

pro_tips: Always use atomic commits when debugging; if the AI suggests a fix, commit it alone so you can revert just that change if it fails.
Use Claude Code's 'read' command on documentation files before asking it to debug, ensuring it understands your specific libraries.
Keep your Antigravity prompts focused on the 'why' rather than the 'how' to encourage the AI to provide architectural improvements.
Use 'git bisect' in tandem with AI if you can't find when the bug was introduced; the AI can help interpret the diffs found during the bisect.
Treat your Git history as a documentation log; if the AI provides a brilliant explanation, paste a summary of it into your commit message.
resources: Git Best Practices for Students: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Once your code is stable, focus on building a comprehensive test suite. Automated tests are the best insurance policy against recurring bugs in future features.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the workflow used by top-tier developers to handle complex technical debt. Stop struggling with errors and start shipping production-grade applications that stand the test of time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

acceptedAnswer.text: Before touching broken code, create a 'debug' branch. This ensures you can always revert to a functional state, allowing the AI to analyze your changes without the fear of losing progress.
Use Claude Code to read your current directory and the specific error log to gain full context of the environment and dependency issues.
Use Antigravity to generate inputs that push your function to its limits, revealing hidden bugs that standard unit tests might miss.
Apply the fix in a dedicated commit. This allows you to verify the fix independently before merging back into your main development branch.
Validate the fix against your test suite. Once confirmed, squash your debug commits to keep a clean, professional history.
